## 1. Role Justification

The Senior Game Engineer is the engineering backbone of the studio — they architect gameplay systems, lead the engineering team, and own Stages 2, 3, 5, and 6 technical deliverables. This is a P0 hire because without a Senior Game Engineer, the Studio Director cannot execute Stage 2 (Prototype), Stage 3 (Vertical Slice), or Stage 5 (Full Production). They serve as the engineering chapter lead for the gameplay domain and directly manage 5 direct reports (Sr. Gameplay Eng. Lead, Sr. Engine Eng., Sr. Backend Eng., Rendering Eng., Lead QA Eng.).

**Pipeline Stages Owned:** 2, 3, 5, 6

# Position Specification Document

**Role:** Senior Game Engineer (G7)
**Role Family:** Engineering
**Seniority:** Principal (L3)
**Team:** Casual Games Studio — Engineering Division
**Reports To:** Studio Director (Dr. Marcus Vogel)
**Priority:** P0
**Compensation Band:** $150K–$200K base
**Document ID:** PSD-2026-G7-001
**Generated:** 2026-04-10T08:00:00Z
**Generated By:** CHRO Office (Automated Pipeline)

---

## 1. Role Justification

The Senior Game Engineer is the engineering backbone of the studio — they architect gameplay systems, lead the engineering team, and own Stages 2, 3, 5, and 6 technical deliverables. This is a P0 hire because without a Senior Game Engineer, the Studio Director cannot execute Stage 2 (Prototype), Stage 3 (Vertical Slice), or Stage 5 (Full Production). They serve as the engineering chapter lead for the gameplay domain and directly manage 5 direct reports (Sr. Gameplay Eng. Lead, Sr. Engine Eng., Sr. Backend Eng., Rendering Eng., Lead QA Eng.).

**Pipeline Stages Owned:** 2, 3, 5, 6

---

## 2. Required Competencies (weighted)

| Competency                       | Weight | Minimum Threshold | Assessment Method                       |
| -------------------------------- | ------ | ----------------- | --------------------------------------- |
| Game Engineering Architecture    | 25%    | ≥ 4/5             | Live system design + coding challenge   |
| Unity/Unreal Expertise           | 20%    | ≥ 4/5             | Technical deep-dive interview           |
| C++/C# Proficiency               | 15%    | ≥ 4/5             | Coding challenge (2 problems, 90 min)   |
| Mobile Optimization              | 15%    | ≥ 4/5             | Performance profiling case study        |
| Multiplayer / Networking         | 10%    | ≥ 3/5             | System design exercise                  |
| Engineering Leadership (5+ team) | 10%    | ≥ 4/5             | Behavioral interview + reference checks |
| Code Review & Mentoring          | 5%     | ≥ 3/5             | Code review exercise                    |

---

## 3. Must-Have Qualifications

- 8+ years game engineering experience
- Unity and/or Unreal Engine mastery
- C++ and C# proficiency at expert level
- Mobile game optimization experience (memory, CPU, GPU, battery)
- Multiplayer/networking experience
- System architecture design for real-time interactive applications

---

## 4. Compensation Structure

| Component         | Range / Value                                      |
| ----------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| Base Salary       | $150K–$200K (positioned at 75th percentile: $175K) |
| Performance Bonus | Up to 15% of base                                  |
| Equity            | 0.20%–0.30% (Principal tier)                       |
| Signing Bonus     | Up to $30K (if leaving unvested equity)            |
| Relocation        | Up to $15K                                         |
| Conference Budget | $7K/year                                           |

---

## 5. Sourcing Channels (prioritized)

| Channel                               | Priority | Quality Weight |
| ------------------------------------- | -------- | -------------- |
| Niantic, Supercell, Mojang alumni     | 1        | 1.5×           |
| GDC Engineering Track speakers        | 2        | 1.3×           |
| Unity/Unreal open-source contributors | 3        | 1.2×           |
| Referrals from CTO/Studio Director    | 4        | 1.5×           |
| LinkedIn passive sourcing             | 5        | 1.0×           |

---

## 6. Assessment Battery

| Assessment                       | Format                       | Duration | Weight |
| -------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | -------- | ------ |
| Coding Challenge                 | 2 problems, sandboxed        | 90 min   | 20%    |
| Live System Design               | Progressive-constraint model | 45 min   | 25%    |
| Code Review Exercise             | PR with known defects        | 60 min   | 15%    |
| Simulated Panel Interview        | AI panel, signal questions   | 45 min   | 20%    |
| Behavioral / Culture Add         | STAR method                  | 30 min   | 10%    |
| Technical Documentation Exercise | Async writing                | 48h      | 10%    |

---

## 7. Success Criteria

| Milestone   | Criteria                                                                                                    |
| ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Day 1**   | Engine access granted, dev environment running, first code review completed                                 |
| **Week 1**  | First PR merged; architecture sketch for Stage 2 prototype drafted                                          |
| **Month 1** | Core gameplay architecture implemented; prototype playable with core loop                                   |
| **Month 3** | Engineering team fully ramped; Stage 3 vertical slice architecture complete; all 5 direct reports onboarded |

---
